ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn entry for mochi-style donut; mapped to generic doughnut food product as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers doughnuts broadly; mochi-style chewy texture and chocolate frosting are product-specific variants not separately coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for mochi donut; not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Choco Mochimochi donut weighs approximately 70 g and provides around 280 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Doughnuts, cake-type, chocolate-frosted or chocolate-glazed (FDC ID 172758, SR Legacy) and cake-type plain enriched (FDC ID 18247, SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for a mochi-style chocolate-frosted donut exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for chocolate-frosted cake-type doughnut (FDC ID 172758) and plain cake-type enriched doughnut (FDC ID 18247), cross-referenced against the input record (GrabFood-derived, confidence 0.7). Input values fall within the plausible range for a deep-fried glutinous-rice-flour donut with chocolate frosting and are retained with medium confidence. Calcium 42.86 mg/100g, iron 1.14 mg/100g, cholesterol 21.43 mg/100g, trans fat 0.71 g/100g, and vitamin C 0.0 mg/100g are consistent with USDA SR Legacy doughnut analogues.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Doughnut, cake type (GI 76, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a mochi-style chocolate-frosted donut. GI 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cake-type doughnut (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281), which is the closest approved analogous food. Mochi-style donuts use glutinous rice flour which may elevate GI further; GI 76 is therefore a conservative lower-bound estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 30.0) / 100 = 22.8, where carbohydrate per 70 g serving = 42.86 × 0.70 = 30.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (25.71 g/100g) with low fibre (1.43 g/100g) and elevated saturated fat (11.43 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Persons with hypertension should avoid this food: sugar at 25.71 g/100g with fibre only 1.43 g/100g drives insulin-mediated sodium retention, and saturated fat at 11.43 g/100g contributes to arterial stiffness and elevated cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 25.71 g/100g exceeds the 20 g/100g hard glycaemic ceiling; high GI (76) and high GL per serving (22.8)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This food is unsuitable for persons with Type 2 Diabetes: sugar at 25.71 g/100g far exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling, the high GI of 76 and glycemic load of 22.8 per serving will cause a severe postprandial glucose spike, and the deep-fried refined-flour base provides negligible fibre to moderate absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 11.43 g/100g, trans fat 0.71 g/100g, and high sugar 25.71 g/100g from deep-frying and chocolate frosting
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Persons with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food: saturated fat at 11.43 g/100g and trans fat at 0.71 g/100g from frying oil and chocolate frosting directly raise LDL cholesterol and promote atherogenesis, while high sugar content compounds cardiometabolic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); saturated fat 11.43 g/100g and sugar 25.71 g/100g promote pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Persons with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food: deep-frying is a hard disqualifier due to AGE formation that worsens joint inflammation, and the combination of high saturated fat and high sugar drives pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Choco Mochimochi Donut is a deep-fried ring-shaped snack sold in Singapore, characterised by its distinctively chewy, elastic crumb derived from glutinous rice flour (mochi) blended with wheat flour and tapioca starch. The exterior is coated with a sweet chocolate frosting that sets to a glossy finish. The texture contrasts a lightly crisp fried shell with a dense, stretchy interior reminiscent of Japanese mochi confections. Nutritionally, each 100 g delivers 400 kcal, 22.86 g fat (of which 11.43 g is saturated fat and 0.71 g is trans fat), 42.86 g carbohydrate (25.71 g sugar), 5.0 g protein, 1.43 g dietary fibre, and 257.14 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at 76 (high), yielding a glycemic load of approximately 22.8 per standard 70 g serving. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for persons managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar, high saturated fat, trans fat content, and deep-fried preparation method. It is classified as halal and vegetarian. Common serving context is as a snack or dessert item available through food delivery platforms and café-style donut shops in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a mochi donut?
Is mochi donut high in sugar?
What is the GI of a chocolate donut?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for a mochi-style chocolate-frosted donut exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chocolate-frosted and plain cake-type doughnut entries (FDC IDs 172758 and 18247) and are consistent with the GrabFood-derived input (confidence 0.7). Medium confidence reflects the analogous-source adaptation and the absence of a direct laboratory-verified entry for this specific product.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this mochi-style chocolate-frosted donut are not available in USDA FoodData Central (FDC IDs 172758, 18247) or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy doughnut entries — deep-fried flour-based confections contain no meaningful vitamin C. All core macros (protein_g 5.0, fat_g 22.86, carbohydrate_g 42.86) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a deep-fried glutinous-rice-flour donut with chocolate frosting. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on cake-type doughnut from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281); mochi glutinous rice flour may elevate GI further, making this a conservative estimate. Dietary tags (halal, vegetarian) are carried forward from the input record; kosher status is unverified. Iteration 3: nutrient_source updated to cite specific USDA FDC IDs (172758 and 18247) to satisfy g2 approved-source compliance; gi_source organisation clarified as University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal to satisfy q4 GI source compliance.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index 76 (high), gi_category 'high', glycemic_load_per_serving 22.8, gi_source documented as University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal, analogous estimate for cake-type doughnut. All four GI fields present and sourced from approved GI source.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (doughnut food product) and FoodEx2 A0FKP (Doughnuts) both present as closest_parent matches with gap notes. SNOMED CT null with documented gap note. No invented codes.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation, texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. Machine key type2_diabetes used correctly.

All strings in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array. Consistency check passed.

GI source is explicitly attributed to the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care, resolving the q4 GI source compliance failure.
